You are researching the C&C Generals Zero Hour source code to help build a headless engine for AI training. The source is at /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot/cczh-source-code/.

## Goal
Produce detailed documentation of the source code so an engineer can:
1. Build the headless engine (strip rendering, run game logic only)
2. Extract game state (units, buildings, resources, fog of war)
3. Inject actions (move, attack, build, train units)

## What to investigate

### 1. Top-level structure
- What's in Generals/ vs GeneralsMD/ (GeneralsMD = Zero Hour expansion?)
- What's in Code/GameEngine/, Code/GameEngineDevice/, Code/Main/, Code/Libraries/

### 2. GameEngine modules
- List all subdirs under Code/GameEngine/Source/ and Code/GameEngine/Include/
- Focus on: AI/, Logic/, Object/, GameNetwork/, Common/, GameLogic/

### 3. Key files to find and read (briefly):
- Game tick / main loop: find the main game loop, identify where LOGICFRAMES_PER_SECOND is defined
- Entity/Object system: how are units represented? Find base Object/Unit class
- Player state: find where money, power, supply are tracked
- Action injection: find how commands/orders are issued (CommandList, OrderType, etc.)
- Fog of war: find FogOfWar or PartitionManager

### 4. GameEngineDevice
- What's in here? Is this the rendering/DirectX layer that needs to be stripped?
- List its subdirectories

### 5. Rendering dependencies
- How tightly coupled is game logic to rendering?
- Are there any existing stubs or null device patterns?

## Output format
Return a structured summary covering all 5 areas above. Be specific: include file paths, class names, function signatures where found. This will be written to documentation.

Keep it thorough but focused on what matters for headless engine + AI integration.

content.text: Good! Both Generals and GeneralsMD have the same structure. Let me check if GeneralsMD is indeed the Zero Hour expansion by looking at the README:

content.content:
1	
2	# Command & Conquer Generals (inc. Zero Hour) Source Code
3	
4	This repository includes source code for Command & Conquer Generals, and its expansion pack Zero Hour. This release provides support to the Steam Workshop for both games ([C&C Generals](https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/browse/?appid=2229870) and [C&C Generals - Zero Hour](https://steamcommunity.com/workshop/browse/?appid=2732960)).
5	
6	
7	## Dependencies
8	
9	If you wish to rebuild the source code and tools successfully you will need to find or write new replacements (or remove the code using them entirely) for the following libraries;
10	
11	- DirectX SDK (Version 9.0 or higher) (expected path `\Code\Libraries\DirectX\`)
12	- STLport (4.5.3) - (expected path `\Code\Libraries\STLport-4.5.3`)
13	- 3DSMax 4 SDK - (expected path `\Code\Libraries\Max4SDK\`)
14	- NVASM - (expected path `\Code\Tools\NVASM\`)
15	- BYTEmark - (expected path `\Code\Libraries\Source\Benchmark`)
16	- RAD Miles Sound System SDK - (expected path `\Code\Libraries\Source\WWVegas\Miles6\`)
17	- RAD Bink SDK - (expected path `\Code\GameEngineDevice\Include\VideoDevice\Bink`)
18	- SafeDisk API - (expected path `\Code\GameEngine\Include\Common\SafeDisk` and `\Code\Tools\Launcher\SafeDisk\`)
19	- Miles Sound System "Asimp3" - (expected path `\Code\Libraries\WPAudio\Asimp3`)
20	- GameSpy SDK - (expected path `\Code\Libraries\Source\GameSpy\`)
21	- ZLib (1.1.4) - (expected path `\Code\Libraries\Source\Compression\ZLib\`)
22	- LZH-Light (1.0) - (expected path `\Code\Libraries\Source\Compression\LZHCompress\CompLibSource` and `CompLibHeader`)
23	
24	
25	## Compiling (Win32 Only)
26	
27	To use the compiled binaries, you must own the game. The C&C Ultimate Collection is available for purchase on [EA App](https://www.ea.com/en-gb/games/command-and-conquer/command-and-conquer-the-ultimate-collection/buy/pc) or [Steam](https://store.steampowered.com/bundle/39394/Command__Conquer_The_Ultimate_Collection/).
28	
29	The quickest way to build all configurations in the project is to open `rts.dsw` in Microsoft Visual Studio C++ 6.0 (SP6 recommended for binary matching to Generals patch 1.08 and Zero Hour patch 1.04) and select Build -> Batch Build, then hit the “Rebuild All” button.
30	
31	If you wish to compile the code under a modern version of Microsoft Visual Studio, you can convert the legacy project file to a modern MSVC solution by opening `rts.dsw` in Microsoft Visual Studio .NET 2003, and then opening the newly created project and solution file in MSVC 2015 or newer.
32	
33	NOTE: As modern versions of MSVC enforce newer revisions of the C++ standard, you will need to make extensive changes to the codebase before it successfully compiles, even more so if you plan on compiling for the Win64 platform.
34	
35	When the workspace has finished building, the compiled binaries will be copied to the folder called `/Run/` found in the root of each games directory. 
36	
37	
38	## Known Issues
39	
40	Windows has a policy where executables that contain words “version”, “update” or “install” in their filename will require UAC Elevation to run. This will affect “versionUpdate” and “buildVersionUpdate” projects from running as post-build events. Renaming the output binary name for these projects to not include these words should resolve the issue for you.
41	
42	
43	## STLport
44	STLport will require changes to successfully compile this source code. The file [stlport.diff](stlport.diff) has been provided for you so you can review and apply these changes. Please make sure you are using STLport 4.5.3 before attempting to apply the patch.
45	
46	
47	## Contributing
48	
49	This repository will not be accepting contributions (pull requests, issues, etc). If you wish to create changes to the source code and encourage collaboration, please create a fork of the repository under your GitHub user/organization space.
50	
51	
52	## Support
53	
54	This repository is for preservation purposes only and is archived without support. 
55	
56	
57	## License
58

60

313		}																																										\
314		inline void prependTo_##LISTNAME(OBJCLASS* o)																				\

342			{																																									\
343				OBJCLASS* originalNext = cur->dlink_next_##LISTNAME();													\
344				cur->dlink_swapLinks_##LISTNAME();																							\
345				prev = cur;																																			\
346				cur = originalNext;																															\
347			}																																									\
348			m_dlinkhead_##LISTNAME.m_head = prev;																							\
349		}																																										\
350	private:																																							\
351		/* a trick: init head to zero */																										\
352		struct DLINKHEAD_##LISTNAME																													\
353		{																																										\
354		public:																																							\
355			OBJCLASS* m_head;																																	\
356			inline DLINKHEAD_##LISTNAME() :																										\
357				m_head(0) { }																																		\
358			inline ~DLINKHEAD_##LISTNAME()																										\
359				{ DEBUG_ASSERTCRASH(!m_head,("destroying dlinkhead still in a list " #LISTNAME)); }				\
360		};																																									\
361		DLINKHEAD_##LISTNAME m_dlinkhead_##LISTNAME;	
362	
363	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
364	#define MAKE_DLINK(OBJCLASS, LISTNAME)	\
365	public:																	\
366		OBJCLASS* dlink_prev_##LISTNAME() const { return m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_prev; }										\
367		OBJCLASS* dlink_next_##LISTNAME() const	{ return m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_next; }										\
368		void dlink_swapLinks_##LISTNAME()																									\
369		{																																													\
370			OBJCLASS* originalNext = m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_next;																			\
371			m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_next = m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_prev;																	\
372			m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_prev = originalNext;																								\
373		}																																													\
374		Bool dlink_isInList_##LISTNAME(OBJCLASS* const* pListHead) const										\
375		{																																													\
376			DEBUG_ASSERTCRASH(!BOGUSPTR(*pListHead) && !BOGUSPTR(m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_next) && !BOGUSPTR(m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_prev), ("bogus ptrs")); \
377			return *pListHead == this || m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_prev || m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_next;		\
378		}																																													\
379		void dlink_prependTo_##LISTNAME(OBJCLASS** pListHead)															\
380		{																																													\
381			DEBUG_ASSERTCRASH(!dlink_isInList_##LISTNAME(pListHead), ("already in list " #LISTNAME));					\
382			DEBUG_ASSERTCRASH(!BOGUSPTR(*pListHead) && !BOGUSPTR(m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_next) && !BOGUSPTR(m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_prev), ("bogus ptrs")); \
383			m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_next = *pListHead;																									\
384			if (*pListHead)																																					\
385				(*pListHead)->m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_prev = this;																				\
386			*pListHead = this;																																			\
387			DEBUG_ASSERTCRASH(!BOGUSPTR(*pListHead) && !BOGUSPTR(m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_next) && !BOGUSPTR(m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_prev), ("bogus ptrs")); \
388		}																																													\
389		void dlink_removeFrom_##LISTNAME(OBJCLASS** pListHead)															\
390		{																																													\
391			DEBUG_ASSERTCRASH(dlink_isInList_##LISTNAME(pListHead), ("not in list" #LISTNAME));			\
392			DEBUG_ASSERTCRASH(!BOGUSPTR(*pListHead) && !BOGUSPTR(m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_next) && !BOGUSPTR(m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_prev), ("bogus ptrs")); \
393			if (m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_next)																													\
394				m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_next->m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_prev = m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_prev;			\
395			if (m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_prev)																													\
396				m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_prev->m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_next = m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_next;			\
397			else																																										\
398				*pListHead = m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_next;																								\
399			m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_prev = 0;																													\
400			m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_next = 0;																													\
401			DEBUG_ASSERTCRASH(!BOGUSPTR(*pListHead) && !BOGUSPTR(m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_next) && !BOGUSPTR(m_dlink_##LISTNAME.m_prev), ("bogus ptrs")); \
402		}																																													\
403	private:																\
404		/* a trick: init links to zero */			\
405		struct DLINK_##LISTNAME								\
406		{																			\
407		public:																\
408			OBJCLASS* m_prev;										\
409			OBJCLASS* m_next;										\
410			inline DLINK_##LISTNAME() :					\
411				m_prev(0), m_next(0) { }					\
412			inline ~DLINK_##LISTNAME()					\
413				{ DEBUG_ASSERTCRASH(!m_prev && !m_next,("destroying dlink still in a list "  #LISTNAME)); } \
414		};																		\

419	#define callMemberFunction(object,ptrToMember)  ((object).*(ptrToMember)) 
420	
421	// ------------------------------------------------------------------------
422	template<class OBJCLASS>
423	class DLINK_ITERATOR
424	{
425	public:
426		// this is the weird C++ syntax for "pointer-to-member-function"
427		typedef OBJCLASS* (OBJCLASS::*GetNextFunc)() const;	
428	private:
429		OBJCLASS* m_cur;
430		GetNextFunc m_getNextFunc;	// this is the weird C++ syntax for "pointer-to-member-function"
431	public:
432		DLINK_ITERATOR(OBJCLASS* cur, GetNextFunc getNextFunc) : m_cur(cur), m_getNextFunc(getNextFunc) 
433		{ 
434		}
435		
436		void advance()
437		{ 
438			if (m_cur)
439				m_cur = callMemberFunction(*m_cur, m_getNextFunc)();
440		}
441		
442		Bool done() const
443		{ 
444			return m_cur == NULL; 
445		}
446	
447		OBJCLASS* cur() const 
448		{ 
449			return m_cur; 
450		}
